concepts.name: Vector
concepts.short_definition: An object with magnitude and direction, often represented as a list of numbers.

concepts.name: Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where addition and scalar multiplication are defined and follow specific axioms.

concepts.name: Scalar
concepts.short_definition: A quantity that has magnitude but no direction, used for scaling vectors.

concepts.name: Vector Addition
concepts.short_definition: The operation of combining two vectors to produce a resultant vector.

concepts.name: Scalar Multiplication
concepts.short_definition: The operation of multiplying a vector by a scalar, changing its magnitude.

concepts.name: Axioms of Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental rules that a set and operations must satisfy to be a vector space.

concepts.name: Subspace
concepts.short_definition: A subset of a vector space that is itself a vector space under the same operations.

concepts.name: Linear Combination
concepts.short_definition: A sum of vectors, each multiplied by a scalar.

concepts.name: Span
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible linear combinations of a given set of vectors.

concepts.name: Basis
concepts.short_definition: A minimal set of vectors that spans a vector space and are linearly independent.
